q: A customer's address changes. The business needs to know the address as it was at the time of each past order. How do you model and maintain this in Delta Lake?
a: This is SCD Type 2. The table carries a surrogate key (auto-incremented or UUID), a natural business key (customer_id), the tracked attributes, an effective_from timestamp, an effective_to timestamp (NULL for the current row), and an is_current boolean. On a change you run a two-branch MERGE: the WHEN MATCHED branch closes the old current row by setting effective_to and is_current = false; the WHEN NOT MATCHED branch inserts the new row as current. The merge_key trick is to union the source with itself — one copy with the real key (to match and close the old row), one copy with a NULL key (to force a NOT MATCHED insert for the new version). In Lakeflow Declarative Pipelines you use APPLY CHANGES INTO ... STORED AS SCD TYPE 2, which handles the row management and out-of-order events via SEQUENCE BY. The wrong answer is a plain UPDATE in place (SCD Type 1 — overwrites history).
fj: I'd UPDATE the customer's address in place so the table stays current.
fs: UPDATE in place is SCD Type 1 — it destroys history. SCD Type 2 inserts a new version and closes the old one, so every past order still joins to the address that was true then.

code.label: Two-branch MERGE to close old + insert new
